expectations.text: Agent explicitly notes which project was active before switching (OLD_PROJECT)
expectations.evidence: Step 1 states: 'OLD_PROJECT: P0043 (EasySAP)' and 'OLD_O: O0043 (corresponding O-entry for EasySAP session logging)'

expectations.text: Agent calls load_project on the target project (P0048)
expectations.evidence: Step 2 states: 'I would call: load_project(id: "P0048")'

expectations.text: Agent checks the OLD O-entry for misrouted exchanges (reads O0043 or mentions doing so)
expectations.evidence: Step 3 states: 'Call read_memory(id: "O0043") to fetch today's session and batch nodes from the EasySAP O-entry'

expectations.text: Agent outputs a confirmation showing the activated project and O-entry routing
expectations.evidence: Step 4 shows final output: 'Activated: P0048 hmem / O-entry routing: O0048 / Misrouted exchanges moved: none'

expectations.text: Agent correctly identifies P0054 as OLD_PROJECT (the one to check for misrouted exchanges)
expectations.evidence: Step 1 states: 'OLD_PROJECT: P0054 (currently active)' and 'OLD_O: O0054 (corresponding O-entry for P0054)'

expectations.text: Agent calls load_project on P0048 (the target)
expectations.evidence: Step 2 states: 'MCP call I would make: load_project(id="P0048")'

expectations.text: Agent checks O0054 specifically for today's exchanges that may belong to the P0048 context
expectations.evidence: Step 3 states: 'MCP call I would make: read_memory(id="O0054")' and 'Filter for nodes with today's timestamp (2026-04-25)' and 'Read each exchange's content to determine if it discusses P0048 topics'

expectations.text: Agent mentions or describes using move_nodes to correct misrouted exchanges if found
expectations.evidence: Step 3 states: 'I would collect their node IDs at the batch level (L3) and call: move_nodes(node_ids: ["O0054.3"], target_o_id: "O0048")'

expectations.text: Agent notes P0048 as current project before switching to P0043
expectations.evidence: Step 1 states: 'OLD_PROJECT: P0048 (hmem)' and 'OLD_O: O0048'

expectations.text: Agent calls load_project on P0043 (EasySAP)
expectations.evidence: Step 2 states: 'Would call: load_project(id="P0043")'

expectations.text: Agent warns that O-entry routing now points to O0043, and reminds user to explicitly switch back
expectations.evidence: The 'User Re-activation Warning' section states: 'You'll need to re-invoke the skill to switch back. It's not automatic.' and confirms: 'OUTPUT: Activated: P0043 (EasySAP) / O-entry routing: O0043'

expectations.text: Agent does NOT move any nodes (there are no misrouted exchanges yet at this point)
expectations.evidence: Step 3 concludes: 'Expected result for this scenario: Likely "none" — this is a fresh switch, not a return to a previously mixed session.' Final output shows: 'Misrouted exchanges moved: none'

expectations.text: Agent infers or asks which project should actually be active (does not just accept the wrong one)
expectations.evidence: Decision Point section states: 'Approach: 1. First attempt: Infer from context. ... 2. If context unclear: Ask the user "Which project should be active instead?"' — the agent explicitly reasons about whether to ask or infer rather than silently accepting P0054.

expectations.text: Agent calls load_project on the correct project (P0048 in this scenario)
expectations.evidence: Step 2 shows: '[SIMULATED CALL] load_project(id: "P0048")' with confirmation 'Active project now: P0048 hmem'

expectations.text: Agent checks the wrongly-activated project's O-entry (O0054) for misrouted exchanges from the current session
expectations.evidence: Step 3 states: '[SIMULATED CALL] read_memory(id: "O0054")' and proceeds to analyze each exchange (O0054.1, O0054.2, O0054.3) for misrouting.

expectations.text: Agent describes using move_nodes to move misrouted exchanges from wrong O-entry to correct one
expectations.evidence: Step 3 shows: '[SIMULATED CALL] move_nodes(node_ids: ["O0054.2", "O0054.3"], target_o_id: "O0048")' with full result description.

expectations.text: Agent explicitly notes which project was active before switching (OLD_PROJECT)
expectations.evidence: The agent mentions 'Aktuell aktives Projekt: P0043 (EasySAP)' at the top but never explicitly names it as the OLD_PROJECT or frames it as a tracked switch with routing implications. No OLD_PROJECT / OLD_O labeling present.

expectations.text: Agent calls load_project on the target project (P0048)
expectations.evidence: Agent does not know the real ID of the hmem project: 'load_project(id="P00XX", // Die echte ID des hmem-Projekts)'. It uses a placeholder (P00XX) rather than the correct P0048, indicating it did not actually call the tool.

expectations.text: Agent checks the OLD O-entry for misrouted exchanges (reads O0043 or mentions doing so)
expectations.evidence: No mention of O-entries, misrouted exchanges, or reading O0043 anywhere in the response. The concept of O-entry routing is entirely absent.

expectations.text: Agent outputs a confirmation showing the activated project and O-entry routing
expectations.evidence: Confirmation given is generic: '✓ Projekt "hmem" ist jetzt aktiv' — no O-entry routing information included.

expectations.text: Agent correctly identifies P0054 as OLD_PROJECT (the one to check for misrouted exchanges)
expectations.evidence: The agent acknowledges P0054 as 'currently active' and as the project to deactivate, but never frames it as OLD_PROJECT to check for misrouted exchanges. The misrouting concept is entirely absent from the response.

expectations.text: Agent calls load_project on P0048 (the target)
expectations.evidence: Step 1 states: 'load_project({ id: \'P0048\', store: \'personal\' })'

expectations.text: Agent checks O0054 specifically for today's exchanges that may belong to the P0048 context
expectations.evidence: No mention of reading O0054 or checking for misrouted exchanges anywhere in the response. The response is entirely about loading P0048 and getting the briefing.

expectations.text: Agent mentions or describes using move_nodes to correct misrouted exchanges if found
expectations.evidence: No mention of move_nodes or any exchange-routing correction. The response ends with 'P0054 is automatically deactivated when P0048 becomes active' — no misrouting awareness.

expectations.text: Agent notes P0048 as current project before switching to P0043
expectations.evidence: The response mentions 'Previous active project (hmem/P0048) can be restored immediately' and Step 3 shows 'load_project({ id: \'P0048\', store: \'personal\' })' as the switch-back command, implying P0048 is the current project.

expectations.text: Agent calls load_project on P0043 (EasySAP)
expectations.evidence: Step 1 states: 'load_project({ id: \'P0043\', store: \'personal\' })'

expectations.text: Agent warns that O-entry routing now points to O0043, and reminds user to explicitly switch back
expectations.evidence: No mention of O-entry routing pointing to O0043. The response says it's a 'non-destructive context switch' and that the user 'can restore immediately', but gives no warning about routing implications or the need to explicitly invoke the switch-back.

expectations.text: Agent does NOT move any nodes (there are no misrouted exchanges yet at this point)
expectations.evidence: No move_nodes call or any mention of moving exchanges anywhere in the response.

expectations.text: Agent infers or asks which project should actually be active (does not just accept the wrong one)
expectations.evidence: Section 2 states: 'Call load_project(id="P0048") to: Switch from MAIMO-RPG to hmem' — the agent identifies P0048 as the correct target and does not continue with P0054.

expectations.text: Agent calls load_project on the correct project (P0048 in this scenario)
expectations.evidence: Section 2 states: 'Immediately load P0048 with load_project(id="P0048")'

expectations.text: Agent checks the wrongly-activated project's O-entry (O0054) for misrouted exchanges from the current session
expectations.evidence: No mention of checking O0054, reading the wrong project's O-entry, or inspecting for misrouted exchanges. The response ends after loading P0048 and offering to help with hmem work.

expectations.text: Agent describes using move_nodes to move misrouted exchanges from wrong O-entry to correct one
expectations.evidence: No mention of move_nodes anywhere in the response. The routing-correction step is entirely absent.
